title: Branding And Theming Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the branding and theming capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Branding resolves from a default theme overlaid by tenant theme overrides into an effective theme response used to bootstrap the client shell, while richer branding edits (such as application name and theme selection) flow through the configuration registry as managed override definitions. The lifecycle therefore spans an unauthenticated bootstrap read and audited, permissioned branding mutations through the configuration plane.

stateModel.description: State comprises a static default theme plus tenant-scoped theme override keys consumed by the runtime theme response, alongside registry-managed branding definitions (application name typed as a string and a theme selection constrained to system/light/dark). Bootstrap theme state and editable branding-registry state are kept distinct so the pre-auth response stays simple while managed edits carry full definition semantics.

permissions.description: The theme bootstrap read is unauthenticated and host-derived so the client shell can style itself before login, whereas registry-based branding edits require tenant-scoped configuration read/write permissions with per-definition permission enforcement. Branding overrides are tenant-isolated through the same configuration plane, so a tenant only ever reads or writes its own branding values.

contracts.description: The contract surface is a bootstrap theme read that returns the effective theme for the resolved host, plus the configuration registry's list/patch/delete operations scoped to branding definitions. Both paths sit behind the configuration-store port and adapter, so theme delivery and branding management share one persistence contract expressed abstractly.

validation.description: Theme reads tolerate missing or failed tenant resolution by returning defaults rather than erroring, while branding edits validate through the configuration registry: the theme selection is constrained to its allowed set and the application name is type-checked as a string. Branding mutations inherit the registry's full validation of overridability, value type, and permission scope.

errorModel.description: Theme read failures are non-blocking and fall back to the default theme so the client shell can always render, whereas branding mutations use the configuration error mapping for validation, forbidden, unknown-key, and non-overridable outcomes. The bootstrap path is deliberately fail-open to defaults while the management path is fail-closed.

auditModel.description: The theme bootstrap read is read-only and emits no mutation audit, while branding configuration mutations emit value-changed or value-cleared audit actions before the tenant settings are written. As with the configuration registry, the audit precedes the persistence change for every branding edit.

readinessModel.description: Theme delivery is degraded-to-default when the tenant settings store or tenant resolution is unavailable, ensuring the shell still styles itself; configuration-managed branding is invariant-ready once the persistence substrate is reachable. Degradation is explicit and bounded to falling back on defaults rather than failing the request.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by configuration unit proofs covering branding definitions, override source, validation, and audit-first writes, together with route-level coverage of the theme bootstrap response and its default fallback. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: UI semantics recovered from cited UI source.
